evidenceStatus.notes: Pi is the only v1 live adapter target; this row declares the typed manifest contract and does not claim live runtime execution.

versionCompatibility.harnessVersionRange: unknown until I-14B/live runtime records installed pi version
versionCompatibility.resourceFormatVersion: Agent Skills standard plus pi-specific prompt/template/extension/package resource formats

compatibilityEvidence.source: DL-06 pi mechanisms and explicit limitations
compatibilityEvidence.notes: Design evidence is known; live runtime compatibility remains I-14B pending-live.

evidence.notes: All six locked skills map to pi Agent Skills slash commands.

skillsCommandsSurface.downstreamConsequence: Downstream lanes may consume the typed mapping, but I-14B owns actual file generation/runtime loading.

evidence.source: DL-03 skill protocols + DL-06 pi skills decision
evidence.notes: The six locked skills are generated as Agent Skills-compatible SKILL.md resources.

evidence.source: DL-03 skill protocols + DL-06 pi skills decision
evidence.notes: The six locked skills are generated as Agent Skills-compatible SKILL.md resources.
Pi prompt templates are `.pi/prompts/*.md` markdown files with filename-derived slash commands and frontmatter/arguments.

promptTemplateSurface.details: Reusable slash-command prompt templates may be declared in `.pi/prompts/<name>.md`.
promptTemplateSurface.downstreamConsequence: Downstream generation must validate template family metadata before writing files.

evidence.notes: Pi TypeScript extensions can register commands/tools and subscribe to events; deterministic gates must not be advisory-only.

hookEventSupport.details: Optional `.pi/extensions/**` TypeScript extensions can provide commands, tools, hooks/events, permission gates, and dynamic resources.
hookEventSupport.downstreamConsequence: Executable extension files require DL-22 trust/security metadata and later runtime proof.

evidence.notes: Pi does not ship built-in subagents; subagent behavior is extension/package-built only.

subagentCapability.details: No built-in subagent claim is made in I-14A.
subagentCapability.downstreamConsequence: Any subagent-dependent lane must wait for generated extension/package implementation and live proof.

evidence.notes: Pi does not ship built-in plan mode; plan-mode-like behavior is extension/package-built only.

planModeCapability.details: No built-in plan-mode claim is made in I-14A.
planModeCapability.downstreamConsequence: Any plan-mode-dependent lane must wait for generated extension/package implementation and live proof.

evidence.notes: Pi loads AGENTS.md and optional CLAUDE.md context files subject to trust and load timing.

contextFileConventions.details: Context file families are `AGENTS.md` and optional `CLAUDE.md`, with generated content preserving project-owned instructions.
contextFileConventions.downstreamConsequence: Downstream writers must use the generated-file manifest and DL-22 trust fields before emitting context files.

evidence.notes: Interactive mode is user-facing operation.

interactive.downstreamConsequence: No automation may infer machine success from prose.

evidence.notes: `pi -p`/`--print` is available for one-shot prompts.

printMode.details: One-shot prompt mode can be used by future runtime witnesses where safe.
printMode.downstreamConsequence: Future runtime lanes must validate machine-readable outputs where they rely on automation.

evidence.notes: `--mode json` provides event-stream observation.

jsonMode.details: JSON mode is the preferred observation surface for event-stream runtime proof.
jsonMode.downstreamConsequence: I-14A does not execute live pi runtime; I-14B owns it.

evidence.notes: RPC `get_commands` can observe generated commands.

rpcMode.details: RPC command listing is a preferred real-boundary observation point.
rpcMode.downstreamConsequence: I-14A proves manifest API consumption only; live pi RPC is pending I-14B.

evidence.notes: SDK/ResourceLoader APIs can observe generated resources.

sdk.details: SDK resource loading can be used by future live witnesses.
sdk.downstreamConsequence: I-14A does not claim SDK runtime loading.

evidence.notes: The generated-file manifest enumerates every permitted pi family and its owner/trust/version/consumer fields.

generatedFiles.downstreamConsequence: Downstream lanes must validate the manifest before generating pi files; malformed or missing family metadata blocks.

evidence.notes: Pi packages may declare resources through a package.json `pi` key; trust/update behavior must be explicit.

packageDistribution.details: Primary v1 generation uses project-local files; optional package distribution is declared but not generated in I-14A.
packageDistribution.downstreamConsequence: Package distribution requires explicit generated-file manifest metadata and later lane implementation.
packageDistribution.trustUpdatePolicy: Project-local files require project trust; package install/update/trust behavior must be explicit before use.

evidence.notes: Adapter declares project trust, executable extension, command policy, sandbox non-claims, credential, destructive-operation, and external-integration defaults.

securityTrust.trustBoundary: Project-local pi resources and TypeScript extensions run only after trust; I-14A claims no OS/network/process sandbox beyond policy declarations.

evidence.notes: Actual package API is consumed by fixture generator/validator and persisted downstream summary; live pi runtime remains out of lane.

selection.reason: Ready only as a typed manifest/API contract for downstream consumers; create/import selected-harness behavior belongs to I-15A.
